description_long: This triumphal arch features five prominent archways, each framed by reddish-brown masonry and supported by classical columns with ornate capitals. The central archway is slightly wider and is flanked by two pairs of smaller arches, all set on a gray stone base. The upper portion of the structure is defined by a series of rounded arches, creating a rhythmic pattern across the top. The central section behind the main arch is filled with brickwork, while the side arches remain open, allowing passage through. The columns appear to be made of stone or concrete, contrasting with the brick and masonry of the arches. The overall design is reminiscent of Roman architectural styles, with a focus on symmetry and decorative detailing.
description: A reddish-brown masonry triumphal arch with five archways, classical columns, and a brick-filled central section. The structure features ornate capitals and a rhythmic series of rounded arches on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five archways, four columns, and the central brick-filled section.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the arch and the layered masonry construction.
description_view_2: Rear view with three visible archways, two columns, and the central brick-filled section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the structure's depth and solid sides.
